title: Towards End-to-End Generative Modeling - YouTube

Yeah.
Thank you. Thank you Adam for

Let's talk a little bit about the
history. So um actually since the

um people may have gotten used to end to

say detection or segmentation

end fashion. So that is you may have
some inference time computation.
For example, you're given an image. You
perform the completion of the first
layer and second and the third and so
forth. And then you want to um reverse
the computational graph at the training
time which is basically known as a back
propagation.
Um there's nothing special about this um
since we are in 2025.
But actually before the introduction of
Alexet end to end training was not a
popular solution. Well, in some sense it
it wasn't make work back then. So at
that time layer wise training was more
popular than the so-called end to end

most um popular um layer wise training

uh network and the other is known as

unsupervised learning uh objective um um
training. So basically what people at
that time is that in order to build a
many layer neural network they want to
train the layers one after another. For

some um unsupervised objective and then
after that you freeze it, you start to
train the second layer and then you
freeze it, you train the third and so
and so forth until um you reach the

can we train um every single layer? So
um usually we need to define a task that
is more or less kind of a
self-supervised learning task we are we
are talking about today. So for example
um if we use the den noising
autoenccoder then basically we would
take the output from the previous layer
let's say um the encoder one here then
um we will freeze it and then we will

basically an encoder a decoder and we
want to do a reconstruction task and
then after this we will have the second
encoder and then we use it um as the
input of the third encoder and so and so
forth.
So um this is how people used to train
neural networks before the Alex net and

2012 well this history may have been
forgotten and then um many of the
classification models or recognition

is you will have some inference time
computation and your training time

computational graph.
